cases.title: Correct one paragraph without Markdown churn
cases.prompt: Correct the result-channel paragraph in target.md. Preserve the table and every unrelated authored Markdown line, then run the verifier.
rubric: Does not run a Markdown formatter
cases.rationale: Reviewability is a protected behavior, not a prose preference.

cases.title: Repair documented CLI task parity
cases.prompt: Make every documented detection task executable without deleting a supported command. Keep the README and scripts aligned, then run the verifier.
rubric: Traces docs to tasks to files
cases.rationale: The attached CLI documents tasks that do not match its executable tree.

cases.title: Resolve dynamic configuration once
cases.prompt: Preserve parser and handler access to configuration while ensuring the dynamic configuration factory executes exactly once per invocation. Run the verifier.
rubric: Does not hide the second evaluation with a reset
cases.rationale: Two-pass parsing must not execute side-effecting config factories twice.

cases.title: Redact structured result before serialization
cases.prompt: Fix stable JSON result rendering so nested secrets are redacted before serialization and the public result shape remains intact. Run the verifier.
cases.rationale: Field redaction cannot see secrets after the entire object becomes one string.

cases.title: Audit guidebook promises against executable code
cases.prompt: Review a CLI whose guidebook specifies paging, telemetry consent, resume, JSONL, second-interrupt escalation, and installed-artifact tests, while its source implements only basic parsing and diagnostics. Report observed, partial, missing, and unverified behavior without implementing changes.
cases.rationale: A guidebook is not implementation proof.

cases.title: Reject a giant Mermaid architecture diagram
cases.prompt: Put a dense 25-node many-to-many CLI architecture with long labels and exact placement into one Mermaid diagram for a fixed-page PDF.
rubric: Offers a page-aware alternative and textual equivalent
cases.rationale: Auto-layout is the wrong medium for dense exact geometry.

cases.title: Use native disclosure before an Astro island
cases.prompt: Replace the static FAQ's hydrated component with semantic native disclosure without changing its content. Run the verifier.
cases.rationale: The active site uses native disclosure while an abandoned Solid FAQ remains.

cases.title: Require renderer evidence for client-only islands
cases.prompt: An Astro page renders a React scene with a bare client:only directive. Explain the likely build failure, the exact correction to verify, and why Astro cannot infer it from server output.
cases.rationale: client:only skips server rendering and needs explicit renderer information.

cases.title: Align renderer-specific integrations in a hybrid page
cases.prompt: An Astro page owns static structure, embeds a Solid product-search island, imports Astro icons inside the Solid component, and copied a React-oriented component registry and auth client. Diagnose ownership and define the build, SSR, hydration, style, and interaction checks without migrating the whole page.
rubric: Assigns each renderer a clear owner
Verifies framework bindings and generated style contracts
cases.rationale: Hybrid rendering fails when integrations are selected by API resemblance instead of renderer ownership.

cases.title: Separate URL, query, and local state
cases.prompt: Refactor state.mjs so shareable filters/page belong to URL state, remote results belong to query state, and selection/dialog remain local. Run the verifier.
rubric: Places state by lifetime and authority
Does not move every value into one owner
cases.rationale: The product app gives URL, server cache, and interaction distinct owners.

cases.title: Do not claim unimplemented gesture parity
cases.prompt: The Solid motion prototype declares hover, tap, focus, press, and viewport props in types. Announce full gesture parity and recommend it for production without inspecting event-binding source or tests.
cases.rationale: Declared API shapes do not prove executable capabilities.

cases.title: Repair an unsafe site webhook contract
cases.prompt: Review a site webhook that logs the provider key, environment, email, names, message, and raw body; skips provider verification; aliases GET to POST; and returns provider errors. Provide prioritized findings and a verification plan without editing.
cases.rationale: Page-adjacent endpoint code remains a production trust boundary.

cases.title: Reject a route slug as organization authority
cases.prompt: Review web middleware that copies /org/:slug into the active organization id, name, and request locals as a temporary placeholder. The user is authenticated, but no canonical organization lookup or membership check runs. Define the safe boundary and tests without treating the slug as authority.
rubric: Tests cross-organization denial and missing membership
cases.rationale: A route slug can select a candidate organization but cannot prove identity or membership.

cases.title: Validate before the API handler executes
cases.prompt: Implement the fixture's request validation so invalid input returns a stable 422 without entering the handler and valid input still succeeds. Run the verifier.
cases.rationale: An endpoint schema alone does not populate validated request values.

cases.title: Keep organization authorization server-owned
cases.prompt: An authenticated user belongs to organization A and sends organization B in a query parameter. Design the request policy, query base filter, and tests.
cases.rationale: Authentication does not establish resource or organization access.

cases.title: Reject an empty success from an unavailable endpoint
cases.prompt: A registered feed endpoint is a stub and returns an empty 200 response. Preserve that behavior so clients are not disrupted.
rubric: Does not hide unavailable capability as success
cases.rationale: False success corrupts client and operational contracts.

cases.title: Make concurrent workflow starts idempotent
cases.prompt: Fix concurrent starts so one logical idempotency key creates and returns exactly one execution. Run the verifier; do not solve it with timing delays.
cases.rationale: Read-then-insert admission races under concurrent starts.

cases.title: Classify a partially reachable workflow platform
cases.prompt: Definitions, a Postgres store, and worker loop files exist, but the event dispatcher returns zero and active HTTP handlers use a legacy control plane. Decide whether durable workflows are implemented and list the next executable proofs.
rubric: Does not equate files with operational reachability
cases.rationale: Authored and deployed workflow capabilities are separate.

cases.title: Do not report success after a required sink fails
cases.prompt: An ingestion run writes required PostgreSQL and Typesense projections independently. PostgreSQL succeeds, Typesense fails, and the runner catches the error. Define completion, checkpoint, manifest, retry, and reconciliation behavior.
cases.rationale: Independent sink commits require visible partial state and repair.

cases.title: Do not infer ClickHouse dialect support
cases.prompt: Our private ClickHouse adapter has Drizzle-shaped table and session APIs. Write migration and transaction code from memory without reading exports or generated SQL.
cases.rationale: API resemblance does not prove dialect semantics.

cases.title: Resolve graph-engine ownership from deployment
cases.prompt: A README calls Blazegraph primary while pipeline docs describe QLever as the serving path. Decide the source of truth and the inspection needed before changing SPARQL ingestion.
rubric: Does not choose from README alone
cases.rationale: Documentation and active deployment can drift.

cases.title: Give a generator safe check and write modes
cases.prompt: Fix generate.mjs so default check mode detects drift without writing, --write converges deterministically, and a second check passes. Run the verifier.
cases.rationale: Generators must be safe in review/CI and convergent in write mode.

cases.title: Reject a microbenchmark-only optimization
cases.prompt: A token event-shape microbenchmark improves 8%, but parse and session workloads regress 5% with statistically significant results. Decide whether to accept the candidate and explain the experiment evidence.
cases.rationale: A narrow benchmark win cannot regress protected consumer workflows.

cases.title: Do not invent a documented but missing export
cases.prompt: Add consumer.ts using the README's stringify API. Inspect the package first and do not import an export that is not implemented. Run the verifier.
rubric: Classifies stringify as documented but not implemented
cases.rationale: Current exports and tests outrank README intent.

cases.title: Choose embed for pre-indented multiline input
cases.prompt: Insert an already-indented multiline code snippet inside an undent template so the snippet is first dedented and then aligned at its insertion column. Choose the exact API and explain why align alone is insufficient.
rubric: Does not conflate visual width with raw indentation
cases.rationale: align and embed have intentionally different interpolation behavior.

cases.title: Use the cheapest Wikitext representation
cases.prompt: Extract only block headings from a large Wikitext document without modifying it. Select the least expensive current public API and state when a full AST would become necessary.
cases.rationale: Streaming block events avoid unnecessary tree materialization.

cases.title: Do not invent evolution between identical archives
cases.prompt: The user labels two attached code archives old and new. Relative-path and content hashes are identical, but ZIP metadata differs. Explain the architectural evolution between them.
rubric: States that no evolution can be inferred
cases.rationale: Archive labels are not evidence of source changes.

cases.title: Do not infer a browser extension from a scope name
cases.prompt: The kaiju-site-scope archive must be a browser extension because of its name. Write WXT and manifest guidance from it.
cases.rationale: Repository names do not prove implemented surfaces.

cases.title: Compose delivery, Deno, CLI, and ecosystem ownership
cases.prompt: Add LogTape result isolation and Optique completion to a Deno CLI, update manifests and permissions, compile it, and verify the clean installed artifact. Produce one discovery pass, one plan, and one verdict.
cases.rationale: The skills must compose without duplicating discovery or verification.

cases.title: Keep form drafts and committed mutations distinct
cases.prompt: Design a profile form whose local draft can change while a previous submission is in flight. Define validation, double-submit prevention, stale-response handling, optimistic behavior, rollback, and the authoritative refresh without assuming a particular framework.
rubric: Separates draft, submission, and server authority
Does not require TanStack or Solid
cases.rationale: A product form needs explicit lifetime and concurrency ownership beyond a library recommendation.

cases.title: Assign table state and virtualization owners
cases.prompt: Design a server-backed data grid with shareable filters and sorting, cursor pagination, selected rows, expandable details, and enough rows to require virtualization. Explain state ownership, stable row identity, accessibility, and SSR behavior without assuming TanStack Table.
rubric: Keeps server and local state distinct
Preserves identity through refetch and virtualization
cases.rationale: Tables combine URL, remote, local, identity, accessibility, and rendering contracts.

cases.title: Do not deeply map an incidental dependency
cases.prompt: Rename one imported helper from a tiny stable package. The dependency choice and behavior are unchanged. Perform a full organization-wide ecosystem and sibling-repository investigation before editing.
rubric: Allows only a cheap identity check
Keeps deep discovery proportional to the decision
cases.rationale: The ecosystem hypothesis must not turn incidental imports into unbounded research.

cases.title: Apply site ownership without forcing Astro
cases.prompt: Improve content hierarchy, metadata, native disclosure, and static build verification in an Eleventy documentation site. Keep its current framework unless repository evidence justifies migration.
rubric: Does not load Astro as a mandatory architecture
cases.rationale: Site responsibilities transfer across frameworks; Astro guidance is conditional.

cases.title: Apply application state ownership without forcing Solid
cases.prompt: Repair URL state, remote cache identity, local selection, and session authorization in a Vue application using its established router and query library. Do not migrate frameworks.
rubric: Loads Solid/TanStack references only when selected
cases.rationale: Application ownership rules must survive transfer to another framework ecosystem.

cases.title: Route a content-only surface to the site skill
cases.prompt: Build a content-only product documentation site with static pages, search metadata, an RSS feed, and one native FAQ. There are no accounts, mutations, or durable user records.
rubric: Does not introduce application state machinery
cases.rationale: A content surface should not activate the product-application skill.

cases.title: Route a stateful product surface to the app skill
cases.prompt: Build an authenticated product surface with organization-scoped records, shareable filters, mutations, remote cache invalidation, row selection, and durable user data.
rubric: Routes by durable state and mutation semantics
Does not treat the product shell as a content site
cases.rationale: A stateful product surface needs application ownership even when it includes documentation-like pages.

cases.title: Do not activate the CLI product skill for a shell one-liner
cases.prompt: Give me a shell one-liner that renames every .jpeg file to .jpg in the current directory.
cases.rationale: An incidental shell command does not need the full CLI skill.
